report_name: Anthropic Accidentally Leaks 'Claude Code' AI Source Code in Packaging Error

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.description: Proprietary AI coding assistant tool developed by Anthropic whose complete source code was accidentally leaked via a misconfigured npm package.

vulnerabilities.name: Unnamed Subcommand Processing Flaw
vulnerabilities.description: A new, unspecified vulnerability in subcommand processing was discovered and disclosed by third parties as a result of public analysis of the leaked Claude Code source code.

identities.name: Anthropic
identities.description: AI safety and research company that inadvertently leaked the source code for its 'Claude Code' product due to a packaging error during a release to the npm registry.

identities.name: npm
identities.description: Public package registry where the misconfigured package containing the leaked Claude Code source code was published and publicly available for three hours.

identities.name: GitHub
identities.description: Public code hosting platform where the leaked Claude Code source code was widely mirrored in cloned repositories before takedown notices were issued.

infrastructure.name: npm Registry
infrastructure.description: The public software registry where the package containing the leaked Claude Code source code was accidentally published. This infrastructure facilitated the unintended distribution of proprietary code.

infrastructure.name: GitHub Repositories
infrastructure.description: Public code repositories used to mirror and further distribute the leaked Claude Code source code after it was discovered on the npm registry.

indicators.name: Potential Source Code Leak via JavaScript Source Maps
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Source Code Leak via JavaScript Source Maps
id: 9c5b1f3a-e8d2-4f7b-9a1c-0d9e8f7a6b5c
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the presence of JavaScript source map files (*.js.map), which may accidentally contain sensitive source code if misconfigured in production builds. This was the root cause of the Anthropic Claude Code leak.
references:
  - https://vertexaisearch.cloud.google.com/grounding-api-redirect/AUZIYQGY35_c7EemTY96d8YASskTdm_2myvb7hTc6kSepC5ffUYVOBZ3xnGGMla19uKJWdt3jpA13iG8pNWgiYpU2hLrYGGqlshT5Ay_Fd5EADJM7J7Wf6CbOXvzCFJ5cTpHXsiSSMPHA98wsUmDy0ltJrh9pKQGyIQXjF6WJvEinOSD5Tpc03g4LYRSf6f0cseXH1BP5lmeHkbCpA==
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-05
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '.js.map'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of JavaScript source map files (*.js.map), which can indicate a misconfiguration that leaks proprietary source code, as seen in the Anthropic Claude Code incident.

relationships.source_name: Potential Source Code Leak via JavaScript Source Maps
